In this specific case, the fastest way to control the bleeding was to directly compress the pulmonary artery with a suction irrigator and place a piece of surgical cellulose, Surgicel, directly on the injury.
With manipulation of the lung, the hemostatic agent was displaced and again results in massive bleeding.
At this time, another hemostatic agent was available, the fibrin sealant patch, also known as Everest, and this was applied on the injury and pressure was held.

We complete our wedge resection and remove the specimen with a thoracoscopic retrieval bag.
